biome_observations.soil_condition: Uniformly dark, suggesting high moisture or lack of light reflection; no signs of active growth or fungal colonization.
biome_observations.surface_debris: A silver spoon is present in the T-2 image, indicating human intervention/tool usage. The white rabbit anchor is the only consistent non-soil element.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a silver spoon into the P2/P4 area. No plant growth observed.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Removal of the silver spoon. Continued absence of plant life.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Static state. No change in soil or pot composition.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. There is no evidence of the registered species (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Pothos, Silver Guest). The environment is currently a 'dead' setup.
anomalies: Persistent absence of all registered botanical specimens
White rabbit anchor is the only non-soil object remaining in the current frame
vision_report.narrative_description: My audit confirms a complete systemic loss of all botanical specimens. The sequence shows a static, barren environment. The only notable event was the temporary placement of a silver spoon in the P2/P4 zone, which was subsequently removed. The biome is currently devoid of life, and the 'white rabbit' anchor serves only to highlight the absence of the Pothos (P3) it was intended to accompany.
